title: Cathedral of Notre Dame de Chartres (quiz)
topic_path: Cathedral of Notre Dame de Chartres (quiz)

assessment_items.question_content:
**The spiritual power of this cathedral comes from the relic of the [[☃ dropdown 1]]  of the [[☃ dropdown 2]]. This relic brought pilgrims to the cathedral.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6b3b045c4ccd5d607475c467dd43ba9f.png)
hints: **Pilgrims were especially interested in an article of the Virgin Mary's clothing. The Romanesque cathedral on this site was burned to the ground in 1194 and the relic was feared to be lost. Three days later the tunic was discovered unharmed in the crypt.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The stained glass in Chartres Cathedral reflected the new Gothic focus on [[☃ dropdown 1]] and [[☃ dropdown 2]].**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/aa9e33720ec089bc0ff06b20faa61731.png)
hints: **Gothic artists at this period were interested in both light and geometric order. The facade of the cathedral was organized according to the Golden Ratio.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**In Gothic architecture, light was a symbol for . . .**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The stained glass program was the main feature of the new Gothic cathedral, where light was a symbol for the divine.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The architect of Chartres Cathedral created a new three-part elevation made up of all of the following parts EXCEPT . . .**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/dc0ac18f75d1d5de75db38a9d03388a0.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The architect of Chartres Cathedral created a new three-part elevation: arcade, triforium, and clerestory. Radiating chapels are part of the ground plan, but not the elevation.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The architect of Chartres Cathedral created a new trend in Gothic cathedrals by including all of the following EXCEPT . . .**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/50b64e152d2553fc333deb92bff18ffa.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The architect used of pointed arches, radiating chapels, and raised the height of the church, starting a new trend in Gothic cathedrals.**

choices.content: raising the height of the church
